history.hypothesis: NFLX exhibits volatile mean-reverting behavior where extreme oversold conditions (price below lower BB, RSI < 30, Stochastic K < 20) reliably revert to the mean. Combining three oversold filters reduces false signals while maintaining enough trades. Exit on mean reversion (BB mid) or RSI recovery ensures capturing the full reversion move without overstaying.

The triple-AND entry condition (BB lower + RSI<30 + Stoch<20) is far too restrictive, yielding only 2 trades. Changing RSI and Stoch from AND to OR logic means we only need ONE oscillator to confirm the BB extreme — this should 2-3x trade count while keeping BB lower as the primary extreme filter. Relaxing RSI threshold from 30 to 40 and Stoch from 20 to 30 further increases signal frequency without sacrificing mean-reversion validity.
